title: Butter-Poached Leeks with Parmesan Foam & Crispy Shallots
summary: We've used a whipped cream dispenser to create Parmesan foam in this impressive side dish. If you prefer, just skip the foam and serve the Parmesan cream as a sauce instead.

method.description: Trim, clean and cut the leeks in half lengthwise. Thinly slice the shallots. Grate the Parmesan.
Melt the butter in a large pan and add the leeks. Cook on a low heat for about 15 mins, basting occasionally, until tender.

Meanwhile, make the Parmesan foam. Warm the double cream in a saucepan and whisk in the grated Parmesan until melted. Season with salt and pepper. Strain the mixture and pour it into a whipped cream dispenser. Screw on the charger and shake vigorously. Set aside (don’t let it get cold).
Toss the shallot slices in flour, shaking off the excess. Fry in hot vegetable oil until crispy and golden. Drain on paper towels and season with salt.
To serve, plate the butter-poached leeks, spray over the Parmesan foam, and garnish with the crispy shallots.

chefs.name: Laurent Dagenais
chefs.summary:
Born and raised in Montreal, Dagenais is a chef by training who worked in the restaurant industry for almost a decade before a snowboarding accident changed his trajectory. “A decade ago my career took a turn that I would have never predicted,” explained Dagenais. “Breaking my arm put my cooking life on hold and that's why, in the midst of the pandemic, I started making videos on the internet.”
 
It was these videos that changed his life, making him an instant social media phenomenon. Reaching over 4+ million followers across his channels, Dagenais' main goal is to share his love of food and inspire people to get in to the kitchen more often.
